title: Gas report - Week 22
summary:
LNG
Without many actual market fixtures taking place, the LNG spot market did see increased rates across the board on all three routes. With the ARB opening expectation, there will be an influx of cargoes and opportunities for traders to begin moving cargo. This has created some uncertainty in the list with ships shown being removed or held back on the expectation that traders will create internal program, which further helps the rate gain.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: The Eastern LPG market has seen a slight boom this week with a rise of $8 from $107.29 to close at $115.429. This is driven mainly by a good steady stream of cargoes and a tighter tonnage list going into mid-June.
